text: After getting home from work, I sat in the dark for almost an hour because I could not find the energy to turn on a light. Even making tea felt like too much effort tonight.
I opened my photo gallery to find one picture and ended up scrolling through old memories, feeling that familiar ache in my chest. The rest of the evening passed in silence.
When my friends invited me out, I typed three different replies and then closed my phone without sending any of them. I wanted company, but I felt too emotionally exhausted to show up.
I finished all my tasks today, but none of it gave me any sense of satisfaction. It felt like moving through thick fog from morning to night.
At lunch, everyone at the table was laughing, and I smiled along so I would not stand out. Inside, I felt disconnected and hollow, like I was watching from far away.
I tried to clean my room to feel better, but halfway through I sat on the floor and just stared at the wall. A wave of grief hit me for reasons I could not fully explain.
This morning I woke up already tired, as if I had spent the whole night carrying something heavy. Even simple decisions felt unusually difficult.
I replayed one old conversation in my mind all day, thinking of what I should have said differently. By evening, I felt defeated and emotionally worn down.
The apartment is clean, my schedule is manageable, and nothing is technically wrong, but I still feel a deep heaviness that does not lift. Every hour feels longer than it should.
I skipped dinner because I was not hungry again, then stayed awake too late thinking about things I cannot change. The night felt long and lonely.
Someone sent me kind words today, and I appreciated it, but the comfort did not really land. My mood stayed low and muted through the whole afternoon.
I stood by the window while it rained and felt an unexpected wave of loss come over me. I could not name it clearly, but it stayed with me for hours.
I keep telling myself tomorrow will be easier, but lately each day starts with the same emotional weight. By noon, I already feel drained.
I canceled plans at the last minute and said I had a headache, which was only half true. Mostly, I just did not have the emotional strength to be around people.
I am functioning, doing what I need to do, and answering messages when I can, but underneath it all I feel numb and quietly sad most of the time.
